name: Aotea Loop
description: A short island loop with mixed road and gravel riding.
difficultyExplanation: Distances are short and the route stays close to settlements with campsites and food access. Gradients can be punchy, but overall logistics are simple and forgiving.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The central ridge track is the steepest part of the loop and can become very muddy after rain, making some sections hike-a-bike.
routeSummary: Aotea Loop is a easy bikepacking route covering about 92.1 km, commonly ridden over 2 days. A short island loop with mixed road and gravel riding. Riders can expect 60% Gravel / 35% Road / 5% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The central ridge track is the steepest part of the loop and can become very muddy after rain, making some sections hike-a-bike. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 2-day route, we recommend a Gravel Bike to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
